narrative: In FY2024-25, Jindal Stainless became the strongest thematic mover because it ranked 1st on MSME/small-producer sourcing at 11.04%, 1st on value-chain environmental assessment at 100%, and tied 1st on sustainable sourcing at 100%; its weaker point was local sourcing at 41%, below Tata and JSW. Tata remained the most balanced large integrated steel peer: 2nd on MSME sourcing at 10%, 2nd on environmental assessment at 80%, tied 1st on sustainable sourcing at 100%, and 2nd on local sourcing at 63%. JSW led local sourcing at 67.22% and tied for sustainable sourcing, but was weaker on MSME and environmental assessment. SAIL was the laggard overall in FY2025, ranking last on MSME, environmental assessment, sustainable sourcing and local sourcing, despite strong MSE vendor-development disclosures and certified captive-mine/sourcing systems. For Tata management, the FY2025 priority is not policy existence but conversion of supplier programmes into 100% value-based environmental assessment and stronger MSME participation without diluting BF-BOF raw-material security.
